_meta.tagline: Re-engineering humanity's approach to learning in an accelerating world.

milestones.title: The Quiet Phase
milestones.desc: No public announcement. No website. No press. The quiet phase secures lead gifts before the world knows we exist. Standard practice — Yale secured $3.5B before announcing 'For Humanity.'

milestones.title: The Public Phase
milestones.desc: The campaign goes public. Website launches. Events held. Press engaged. The full case statement reaches every major prospect worldwide.
deliverables: Public website and campaign materials live

milestones.title: Close & Capitalise
milestones.desc: All $100M commitments secured. Properties acquired. Legal incorporation across 25 jurisdictions. The foundation is poured.

milestones.title: Build & Launch
milestones.desc: Faculty onboarded. Colleges opened. Students arriving. The university becomes operational. The match lights the fuel.

pillars.title: Place
pillars.subtitle: The 50 Colleges
pillars.desc: A university without a physical place is a YouTube channel. Community requires co-location. Tutorials require rooms. We are not building campuses — we are finding existing buildings and giving them a second life as Colleges. Repurposed convents, warehouses, offices, factories. Each one acquired. Each one owned. Each one permanent.
pillars.detail: Properties are 82 cents of every dollar raised. Even at 50% naming uptake, the gifts cover the property costs. Named gifts are endowments — the property is the asset. Donor names a College; the College owns the building.

pillars.title: Minds
pillars.subtitle: Faculty Launch
pillars.desc: 2,000 faculty must be hired, onboarded, and in place before students arrive. This pillar covers roughly 3 months of faculty compensation before tuition revenue flows. After Day 1, the P&L covers all faculty compensation from tuition. This is a one-time bridge.
pillars.detail: A $5M Distinguished Professorship at 4.5% yield generates $225K/year — enough to sustain one position in perpetuity at career-level compensation. The naming gifts are endowments; the $7M pre-launch cost is the bridge.

pillars.title: Access
pillars.subtitle: Year 1 Scholarships
pillars.desc: $3,000/year is accessible to 90% of qualified students worldwide. The remaining 10% — 10,000 students in Year 1 — need assistance. After Year 1, the $262M annual surplus funds 13,300 scholarships per year. This $5M bridges the first cohort.
pillars.detail: After Year 1, the surplus self-funds scholarships at $40M/year. Your seed gift does not run a programme — it launches a permanent scholarship machine.

pillars.title: Excellence
pillars.subtitle: Research & Inquiry
pillars.desc: Research seed funds, visiting fellowships, the Artemis Press, Centers of Inquiry. The mechanisms that turn a good university into an inevitable one.
pillars.detail: 19 Centers of Inquiry and 42 Active Projects need startup capital: research equipment, fieldwork budgets, database subscriptions, publication subsidies, and the open knowledge infrastructure that powers the 7-year release. After Year 1, faculty time (already compensated) covers most ongoing project work. This $3M is the seed that makes every Center operational from Day 1.

pillars.title: Progress
pillars.subtitle: Innovation & Infrastructure
pillars.desc: Innovation labs, the Global Challenge Fund, technology infrastructure. The bridge between scholarship and the world it serves.
pillars.detail: The university that outlives its founders needs a corpus that outlives its donors. After Year 1, the surplus builds the endowment at $60M/year. $3M at 4.5% = $135K/year in perpetuity from Day 1 — enough to fund 11 full scholarships forever, before any surplus is generated.

naming_opportunities.title: Tier C College
naming_opportunities.desc: Name a College in cities like Kigali, Dhaka, Kampala, Karachi. ~1,100 students. Your name becomes home to the next generation of leaders in your region.

naming_opportunities.title: Tier B College
naming_opportunities.desc: Name a College in major global cities. ~2,000 students. A permanent physical presence bearing your name, serving scholars for centuries.

naming_opportunities.title: Tier A College
naming_opportunities.desc: Name a flagship College in the world's great knowledge capitals. ~2,500 students. The highest-profile naming opportunity outside the Central Nodes.

naming_opportunities.title: Central Node
naming_opportunities.desc: Name one of three Central Nodes — Venice, San Francisco, Singapore. ~5,000 students. The apex of the Artemis network, bearing your name in perpetuity.

naming_opportunities.title: Distinguished Professorship
naming_opportunities.desc: At 4.5% yield, generates $225K/year — sustaining one position in perpetuity at career-level compensation. The naming gift is an endowment.

naming_opportunities.title: Center of Inquiry
naming_opportunities.desc: Name one of 19 Centers of Inquiry — a permanently endowed, independently operating research centre carrying your name.

naming_opportunities.title: Degree Program
naming_opportunities.desc: Name one of 55 degree programs. Your name associated with a specific discipline and every graduate who carries it forward.

naming_opportunities.title: Scholarship Fund
naming_opportunities.desc: Fund one student's full scholarship for four years. $3,000/year × 4 years. The most direct way to change a life.

giving_circles.name: The Founders' Circle
benefits: Name engraved on the university's founding document at Venice Central Node
Permanent seat on Board of Visitors
Private annual dinner with the President at rotating Central Node
Named endowment fund or equivalent naming opportunity
Recognition plaque at all 50 Colleges
Biographical feature in annual campaign report

giving_circles.name: The Guardians' Circle
benefits: Named College or Center of Inquiry
Recognition plaque at all 50 Colleges
Annual Guardian's Lecture — a public lecture series named by you
Annual private briefing with the President
Named endowment fund

giving_circles.name: The Builders' Circle
benefits: Named Professorship, Scholarship Fund, or major program
Recognition plaque at all 50 Colleges
Annual Builder's Report — detailed impact report on your named gift
Invitation to annual gathering
Feature in campaign newsletter

giving_circles.name: The Fellows' Circle
benefits: Named scholarship fund or tutorial room at chosen College
Recognition at chosen College
Annual Fellow's Newsletter from the President
Invitation to regional events

giving_circles.name: Friends of Artemis
benefits: Name in founding Donor Roll (permanent digital and print record)
Digital certificate of founding support
Quarterly progress updates
Invitation to online events

giving_circles.name: The 99
benefits: Waitlist priority for enrollment
Digital recognition on community wall
Monthly community updates
The knowledge that you were first in line

legal_entities.name: Delaware Non-Stock Corporation
legal_entities.key_benefit: Tax-deductible donations in the world's largest philanthropy market ($499B in 2024). Every dollar given reduces the donor's US tax liability.
structure: Non-stock corporation — no shareholders, no owner
Retroactive deductibility within 27 months of incorporation
Required: 3+ independent board members, no single controller

legal_entities.name: Charitable Incorporated Organisation
legal_entities.key_benefit: Gift Aid turns every £1M into £1.25M through HMRC top-up. UK donors receive full tax relief. Aligns with University of London consortium partnership.
structure: CIO or Company Limited by Guarantee
3+ trustees required with UK registered office

legal_entities.name: Fondation under Swiss Civil Code
legal_entities.key_benefit: Endowment management in a neutral jurisdiction with minimal tax drag. Signals institutional permanence to European and Gulf donors. Home to WHO, CERN, IOC.
structure: No capital gains tax on endowment growth

accountability.title: Board Independence
accountability.desc: No single individual controls more than one-third of board seats. Majority independent directors required.

accountability.title: Annual Audit
accountability.desc: Full independent audit by a Big Four firm. Published annually. No exceptions.

accountability.title: Public Reporting
accountability.desc: Every dollar received and disbursed is published in an annual impact report, available to every donor and the public.

donor_segments.desc: 70% of the campaign from 10 donors. This is how founding campaigns work.
20 donors at the $1M–$5M level, building the academic infrastructure.
Hundreds of donors at $10K–$999K, seeding scholarships, rooms, and research.

ways_to_give.title: Online
ways_to_give.desc: Make a one-time or recurring gift by card, PayPal, or cryptocurrency directly on the website. Fast, secure, and immediate confirmation of your contribution.

ways_to_give.title: Bank or Wire Transfer
ways_to_give.desc: Transfer directly from your bank. Suitable for large contributions and international donors.

ways_to_give.title: Cryptocurrency
ways_to_give.desc: Donate Bitcoin (BTC) or Ethereum (ETH) directly to Artemis. Crypto donations are recorded on the public chain, making your contribution a permanent part of the university's founding record.

ways_to_give.title: Securities and Stock
ways_to_give.desc: Donate appreciated stocks, bonds, or mutual funds to avoid capital gains tax while maximising your impact.

ways_to_give.title: Planned Giving
ways_to_give.desc: Include Artemis in your estate plan through a will, trust, or beneficiary designation. Planned gifts create a permanent legacy.

ways_to_give.title: Employer Matching
ways_to_give.desc: Many employers match charitable contributions dollar-for-dollar. Check with your HR department — your gift could be doubled at no additional cost.

ways_to_give.title: Donor-Advised Funds
ways_to_give.desc: Recommend a grant from your donor-advised fund (DAF) to the University of Artemis. A tax-efficient way to support the founding.

ways_to_give.title: In-Kind Contributions
ways_to_give.desc: Donate equipment, technology, library materials, real estate, or professional services. In-kind gifts are valued and recognised.

donors.name: The Nordgren Foundation
donors.msg: Investing in the infrastructure of imagination.

donors.name: Dr. Elena Vasquez
donors.msg: For the students who will change everything.

donors.name: Anonymous Patron
donors.msg: Because knowledge should have no borders.

donors.name: Dr. Robert & Sarah Kimani
donors.msg: For the next generation of African scholars.

donors.name: Sven & Astrid Lindqvist
donors.msg: For the north, and for everywhere.

donors.name: Amara Osei
donors.msg: Every great university starts with a first believer.

donors.name: Maria Santos
donors.msg: Proud to be part of the founding.

tax_jurisdictions.notes: Deductible within tax year; 5-year carryforward. EIN issued upon Form 1023 approval.

tax_jurisdictions.deductibility: Gift Aid: 25% government top-up. Higher-rate relief via self-assessment.
tax_jurisdictions.notes: £1,000 donation costs £750 after basic-rate relief + £250 HMRC top-up.

tax_jurisdictions.deductibility: Tax-deductible; no percentage cap for gifts >$2.
Up to 20% of total income; 5-yr carryforward.

tax_jurisdictions.notes: Geneva & Zurich most favourable for foundations.
One of the most generous regimes globally.
Approved under Inland Revenue Ordinance s88.

tax_jurisdictions.deductibility: No income tax in UAE; Zakat alignment for GCC donors.
tax_jurisdictions.notes: Structured via DIFC/ADGM for institutional donors.

tax_jurisdictions.deductibility: Up to 2% of operating revenue (corporate); limited for individuals.

campus_nodes.name: Venice Central Node
campus_nodes.role: The founding seat — governance, convocation, the founding document archive. Canals and Renaissance palazzi repurposed as the academic heart.

campus_nodes.name: San Francisco Central Node
campus_nodes.role: The technology and innovation seat — proximity to Silicon Valley, the Forge incubator, biotech and computational research.

campus_nodes.name: Singapore Central Node
campus_nodes.role: The Asia-Pacific seat — a neutral convening point for the Eastern hemisphere, fintech and urban futures research.

campus_nodes.name: Tier A Colleges
campus_nodes.role: Flagship colleges in the world's great knowledge capitals — London, Tokyo, Berlin, Paris, Toronto, Sydney, Seoul, New York.

campus_nodes.name: Tier B Colleges
campus_nodes.role: Major global cities across all six continents — Nairobi, Mumbai, São Paulo, Cairo, Istanbul, Buenos Aires, Jakarta, Mexico City, and more.

campus_nodes.name: Tier C Colleges
campus_nodes.role: Regional access colleges — Kigali, Dhaka, Kampala, Karachi, Tashkent, Quito, and emerging knowledge cities.

academic_pillars.title: Epistemology
academic_pillars.desc: How we know what we know. The foundations of inquiry, evidence, reasoning, and the limits of certainty — taught not as a philosophy elective but as the substrate of every discipline.

academic_pillars.title: Computational Thinking
academic_pillars.desc: Not just coding, but the algorithmic decomposition of problems across every field — from literary analysis to epidemiology to constitutional design.

academic_pillars.title: Global Systems
academic_pillars.desc: Climate, finance, migration, supply chains, governance. The interlocking systems that define the 21st century, studied as integrated wholes rather than siloed specialties.

academic_pillars.title: Creative Expression
academic_pillars.desc: The studio, the workshop, the stage. The conviction that making is knowing, and that every graduate must be able to build, write, compose, and perform — not merely analyze.

programs.title: B.S. Computer Science
programs.desc: Integrating deep software systems, data science pipelines, AI/ML, and computational theory with the epistemic foundations of intelligence itself.

programs.title: B.S. International Business
programs.desc: Empowering future executives with skills in global finance, cross-cultural leadership, and the political economy of interconnected markets.

programs.title: B.S. Environmental Science
programs.desc: Analyzing complex global ecosystems, climate systems, and biodiversity with fieldwork across six continents.

programs.title: B.A. Philosophy, Politics & Economics
programs.desc: The integrated study of governance, markets, and ethics — the classical tripos reimagined for a planetary century.

programs.title: B.S. Biotechnology
programs.desc: From gene editing to synthetic biology, with deep integration of bioethics and translational innovation.

programs.title: B.A. Architecture & Urban Design
programs.desc: Designing adaptive, regenerative built environments — studios in six rotating hostel cities.

programs.title: B.S. Data Science & Statistics
programs.desc: The mathematical, computational, and ethical foundations of inference at scale.

programs.title: B.A. Global History
programs.desc: World history as an integrated discipline — not Western civilization plus electives, but a genuinely planetary curriculum.

programs.title: B.S. Cognitive Science
programs.desc: The mind — computational, biological, philosophical — studied as the central mystery of the century.

centers_of_inquiry.name: Synthetic Intelligence
centers_of_inquiry.desc: AI/human symbiotic reasoning, the CoThink project, moral agency in AI.

centers_of_inquiry.name: Cosmological Humanities
centers_of_inquiry.desc: Processing petabytes of telescope data, philosophical astrophysics.

centers_of_inquiry.name: Urban Futures
centers_of_inquiry.desc: Designing cities that are resilient, equitable, and alive — integrating architecture, ecology, computation, and governance.
focus: Adaptive urban design and computational architecture
Smart city systems and digital twins
Equitable housing and inclusive urban development

centers_of_inquiry.name: Biotech & Life Sciences
centers_of_inquiry.desc: From gene editing and synthetic organisms to ecosystem engineering and de-extinction.

centers_of_inquiry.name: Fintech, DeFi & Economics
centers_of_inquiry.desc: Rethinking money, markets, and economic governance for a decentralized, automated, globally interconnected world.

centers_of_inquiry.name: Health & Bioethics
centers_of_inquiry.desc: AI-assisted diagnosis, gene editing ethics, pandemic preparedness, global health justice.
focus: Global health justice and access to care

targets: First 12 properties acquired and repurposing begun
Global recognition — accreditation across all jurisdictions
The Artemis Model published as open blueprint

financial_model.surplus_note: Year 1 break-even. From Year 2: $262M annual surplus (tuition $180M + $120M growth vs $200M faculty lock) funds scholarships, endowment, and expansion. The model is designed to flip from capital-raising to self-sustaining within 12 months of launch.
